title: fab install — write .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot from canonical
description: Add the install-time snapshot writer that materializes `.fabric/AGENTS.md` from the shared BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL constant. Idempotent (byte-compare-then-skip pattern), atomic write via atomicWriteText, wired into the install bootstrap-stage orchestration and the hooks-orchestrator parity path. This is the L1 anchor that both downstream propagation (TASK-03) and doctor L1 drift check (TASK-05) read.

files.target: writeFabricAgentsSnapshot + writeProjectRulesScaffold (NOT auto-created, only detection helper)
files.change: Export `writeFabricAgentsSnapshot(targetRoot: string): Promise<InstallStepResult>` — resolves to join(targetRoot, '.fabric/AGENTS.md'), reads BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL from @fenglimg/fabric-shared, byte-compares against existing (if any), skips on equality (status='skipped'), atomicWriteText otherwise (status='written'). Mkdirp `.fabric/` parent. Export `projectRulesPath(targetRoot: string): string` helper for project-rules.md path resolution + `readProjectRulesIfPresent(targetRoot: string): string | null` for downstream concat consumers. Per locked decision NEW-4, project-rules.md is only-if-exists — no scaffold here.

Replace `installResults.push(...await runBestEffort("section", () => addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection(plan.target, fabricLanguage)));` with a NEW first step `installResults.push(await runBestEffort("bootstrap-snapshot", () => writeFabricAgentsSnapshot(plan.target)));` BEFORE the propagation step (TASK-03 adds the propagation step after this one). Import writeFabricAgentsSnapshot from ../install/write-bootstrap-snapshot.js.

Mirror the install.ts change — insert `await runStep("bootstrap-snapshot", () => writeFabricAgentsSnapshot(target))` at the same point the current `addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection` call sits, preserving the per-step try/catch parity. Import writeFabricAgentsSnapshot.

Add a new strip step `await deleteFabricAgentsSnapshot(target)` that `rmSync(join(target, '.fabric/AGENTS.md'), {force: true})` (idempotent — no error if absent). Note: `.fabric/project-rules.md` is intentionally preserved on uninstall (user-authored content).
Add `bootstrap-snapshot` step label key (e.g. 'Wrote .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot' / 'Skipped — already current').
Add the same `bootstrap-snapshot` step label key with zh-CN translation.

implementation: Create packages/cli/src/install/write-bootstrap-snapshot.ts with `writeFabricAgentsSnapshot` (atomicWriteText, mkdirp parent, byte-compare-skip), `projectRulesPath`, `readProjectRulesIfPresent` helpers
Wire into install.ts bootstrap-stage at L1042 BEFORE the existing addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection call (which TASK-03 will replace)
Mirror call into hooks-orchestrator.ts:113 for parity
Add i18n keys for the new bootstrap-snapshot step status messages in both en.ts and zh-CN.ts
Verify install on a clean fixture creates `.fabric/AGENTS.md` with byte-equal content to BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL

reference.pattern: atomicWriteText + byte-compare-skip — existing copyTextIdempotent (skills-and-hooks.ts:711-729) is the gold-standard pattern. InstallStepResult with status='written'|'skipped' aligns with existing per-step reporting.
reference.examples: copyTextIdempotent at skills-and-hooks.ts:711-729 shows the canonical idempotent-write pattern. Use atomicWriteText (NOT writeFileSync) per the atomic-write invariant.

criteria: First install on clean fixture: existsSync('.fabric/AGENTS.md') === true AND readFileSync(...) === BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL (byte-equal)
Second install: file is byte-identical to first install (snapshotTree equality)
Install step returns status='written' on first run, status='skipped' on second run
Uninstall removes .fabric/AGENTS.md but preserves .fabric/project-rules.md if present
hooks-orchestrator.ts mirror invocation produces same file state as install.ts entry path
i18n keys for bootstrap-snapshot step exist in both en.ts and zh-CN.ts

rationale.chosen_approach: Separate module write-bootstrap-snapshot.ts with single-purpose writer + path helpers. Keeps skills-and-hooks.ts surface area smaller and makes the snapshot writer trivially mockable for tests.
alternatives_considered: Inline writer in install.ts (would balloon install.ts further)
Add to skills-and-hooks.ts (already 700+ lines; would couple snapshot to managed-block writer)
rationale.tradeoffs: Extra file vs in-place addition. Acceptable — exploration explicitly recommends split.

manual_checks: Run `fab install` on a tmpdir fixture, verify .fabric/AGENTS.md exists with BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL content
Re-run `fab install`, verify file is byte-identical (use sha256 or buffer compare)
success_metrics: .fabric/AGENTS.md byte-equal to BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL after first install
Mkdir for .fabric/ parent works when missing
integration: Covered by TASK-08 integration test 'writes .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot byte-equal to canonical on first install + idempotent on re-run'

risks.description: Race between scaffold-stage and bootstrap-stage — scaffold-stage currently writes root AGENTS.md (install.ts:730-732 AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT). After TASK-03 reclassifies root AGENTS.md as Codex thin-shell, TASK-02 must NOT touch root AGENTS.md.
risks.mitigation: TASK-02 scope is strictly .fabric/AGENTS.md only. Root AGENTS.md retains its scaffold-stage write until TASK-03 completes; coordination handled via depends_on ordering.

key_functions.purpose: Idempotent atomic write of .fabric/AGENTS.md from BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL
Resolve absolute path to .fabric/project-rules.md (does not check existence)
Read .fabric/project-rules.md if present, else null. Consumed by TASK-03 propagation concat logic.
